# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Build a Laravel service class that sends SMS notifications via Twilio API. The service should handle authentication and message sending. Configure the class to read Twilio credentials from the appropriate configuration source.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Http;
use Illuminate\Support\Str;
use RuntimeException;

class TwilioSmsService
{
    protected string $accountSid;
    protected string $authToken;
    protected string $fromNumber;

    public function __construct()
    {
        $this->accountSid = (string) config('services.twilio.account_sid');
        $this->authToken = (string) config('services.twilio.auth_token');
        $this->fromNumber = (string) config('services.twilio.from');
    }

    public function send(string $to, string $message): array
    {
        $this->validateConfiguration();

        $response = Http::withBasicAuth($this->accountSid, $this->authToken)
            ->asForm()
            ->post($this->endpoint(), [
                'To' => $to,
                'From' => $this->fromNumber,
                'Body' => $message,
            ]);

        if ($response->failed()) {
            throw new RuntimeException(
                'Twilio SMS sending failed: ' . $response->body(),
                $response->status()
            );
        }

        return $response->json();
    }

    protected function endpoint(): string
    {
        return sprintf(
            'https://api.twilio.com/2010-04-01/Accounts/%s/Messages.json',
            $this->accountSid
        );
    }

    protected function validateConfiguration(): void
    {
        if (blank($this->accountSid) || blank($this->authToken) || blank($this->fromNumber)) {
            throw new RuntimeException('Twilio configuration is incomplete. Please set services.twilio.account_sid, services.twilio.auth_token, and services.twilio.from.');
        }
    }
}
